CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE OF DEC-29-2020 CONTINUED TO FEB-23-2021 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) THE LANDLORD IS ENJOINED FROM COLLECTING FUTURE RENT UNTIL A CERTIFICATE OF OCCUPANCY IS OBTAINED; (2) THE LANDLORD IS ENJOINED FORM OFFERING ANY UNITS IN THE PROPERTY FOR RENT TO NEW/PROSPECTIVE TENANTS UNLESS THE PROPERTY IS BEING OFFERED FOR RENT IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE EXISTING CERTIFICATE OF OCCUPANCY. THE LANDLORD IS NOT PRECLUDED FROM ADDRESSING THE TENANCIES BY ANY MEANS AUTHORIZED BY LAW, E.G. SALAZAR V. MARADEAGA (2010) 10 CAL.APP.4TH SUPP.1; SFRO 37.9(A)(10). SUBJECT TO REVIEW OF THE ORDER. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Jun-24-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alejandro Herrera, Esq., 310-570-0513, appearing for Shayla Shahar, Plaintiff and moving party.

No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order is GRANTED as follows: (1) the landlord is enjoined from collecting future rent until a Certificate of Occupancy is obtained; (2) the landlord is enjoined form offering any units in the property for rent to new/prospective tenants unless the property is being offered for rent in accordance with the existing Certificate of Occupancy. The landlord is not precluded from addressing the tenancies by any means authorized by law, e.g. Salazar v. Maradeaga (2010) 10 Cal.App.4th Supp.1; SFRO 37.9(a)(10). Subject to review of the order. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
